notes: Lesson 24 Part 1 (act 141) 'Rotting Oranges — multi-source grid BFS', 75.8s, 6 cues. Verified example grid RFFFR/FFFFF/FFFFF, 2 rotten sources (0,0)+(0,4), deepest fresh cell (2,2) rots at minute 4 -> answer 4; unreachable fresh -> -1. Uses graph.py grid_board/set_cell/minute_tag/container. Single -ql pass clean; READ start/mid/end each cue.

frames.notes: [start] grid at minute 0: 2 orange rotten sources ringed, 13 green fresh, frontier queue [(0,0),(0,4)] with 'front' marker, 'fresh 13' badge, note '2 sources start together'
[mid] grid at minute 0: 2 orange rotten sources ringed, 13 green fresh, frontier queue [(0,0),(0,4)] with 'front' marker, 'fresh 13' badge, note '2 sources start together'
[end] grid at minute 0: 2 orange rotten sources ringed, 13 green fresh, frontier queue [(0,0),(0,4)] with 'front' marker, 'fresh 13' badge, note '2 sources start together'
[start] one minute: sources popped (blue current), 4 minute-1 neighbours flip orange and join next frontier, 'minute 1' badge, note 'all 4 neighbours flip at once'
[mid] one minute: sources popped (blue current), 4 minute-1 neighbours flip orange and join next frontier, 'minute 1' badge, note 'all 4 neighbours flip at once'
[end] one minute: sources popped (blue current), 4 minute-1 neighbours flip orange and join next frontier, 'minute 1' badge, note 'all 4 neighbours flip at once'
[start] every cell stamped with the minute it flips = BFS layer (0/1/2/1/0 / 1/2/3/2/1 / 2/3/4/3/2), sources ringed rose, 'deepest 4' badge; note 'each number = minutes to nearest source'
[mid] every cell stamped with the minute it flips = BFS layer (0/1/2/1/0 / 1/2/3/2/1 / 2/3/4/3/2), sources ringed rose, 'deepest 4' badge; note 'each number = minutes to nearest source'
[end] every cell stamped with the minute it flips = BFS layer (0/1/2/1/0 / 1/2/3/2/1 / 2/3/4/3/2), sources ringed rose, 'deepest 4' badge; note 'each number = minutes to nearest source'
[start] flip-on-enqueue rule: two rotten neighbours reach one fresh cell; first flips+marks it 'rotten checkmark', second sees 'already rotten — skip'; note 'each cell in the queue exactly once'
[mid] flip-on-enqueue rule: two rotten neighbours reach one fresh cell; first flips+marks it 'rotten checkmark', second sees 'already rotten — skip'; note 'each cell in the queue exactly once'
[end] flip-on-enqueue rule: two rotten neighbours reach one fresh cell; first flips+marks it 'rotten checkmark', second sees 'already rotten — skip'; note 'each cell in the queue exactly once'
[start] fresh reaches 0 at minute 4, deepest cell (2,2) green with '4', 'fresh 0' badge, 'answer = 4 minutes' chip; note 'queue empties with no fresh left'
[mid] fresh reaches 0 at minute 4, deepest cell (2,2) green with '4', 'fresh 0' badge, 'answer = 4 minutes' chip; note 'queue empties with no fresh left'
[end] fresh reaches 0 at minute 4, deepest cell (2,2) green with '4', 'fresh 0' badge, 'answer = 4 minutes' chip; note 'queue empties with no fresh left'
[start] unreachable case: a fresh cell fenced from the source by empty walls stays fresh; 'stuck fresh 1', 'answer = -1' chip
[mid] unreachable case: a fresh cell fenced from the source by empty walls stays fresh; 'stuck fresh 1', 'answer = -1' chip
